confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_e64d0e27-d024-4dba-abc7-f3e59d0abc5f read back as promoted

evidence: narada task review 60 --agent narada-andrey.Kevin refused with Agent not found in roster: narada-andrey.Kevin.
Repair required narada task roster add narada-andrey.Kevin --role architect --capability ... review, then task review 60 succeeded and unblocked task 61.
After closure, narada task work-next --agent narada-andrey.Bob returned task 61.
observations: Operator-surface identity and task roster/review authority can drift after an agent identity migration.
The responsible architect can fail to recognize a pending review as self-owned because task machinery does not project the active operator-surface identity into task authority.
task review emitted a CAPA recommendation whose text referenced rejected findings/rejection even though the verdict was accepted_with_notes.
recommendation: Provide a canonical identity migration/admission path that updates operator-surface identity, task roster projection, role/review authority, and aliases together.
Add a doctor/preflight that reports active operator-surface identity is not admitted to task authority before review/close attempts.
Fix CAPA recommendation wording so accepted review verdicts do not mention rejection-specific rationale.
payload.summary: In narada-andrey, the active architect surface was renamed/bound as narada-andrey.Kevin in operator-surfaces/identities.json, but task review authority still knew only narada-andrey.architect. When Kevin attempted to review task 60 to unblock dependent task 61, narada task review refused with missing_reviewer_identity. This left OSM envelope-context work blocked even though it was directly relevant to current OSM defects.
payload.title: Task authority did not follow operator-surface identity migration
